channel.title: ClawTutor Blog
channel.description: Dev log and updates from ClawTutor — AI tutors that quiz your kids via WhatsApp and reward correct answers with screen time credits.

item.title: First Community Contribution — A Security Fix 🎉
item.description: Five days after launch, @spnmlr found and fixed a shell injection vulnerability in the device control script. This is exactly what open source is for.

item.title: Dictation Mode — Learning Languages with Voice Messages
item.description: ClawTutor's English tutor Nelly can now send voice messages for dictation exercises and vocabulary practice. Real listening comprehension, real spelling practice — no cheating possible.

item.title: "Max, Zeit fürs Quiz!" — Voice Announcements via Bluetooth
item.description: WhatsApp notifications are easy to ignore. A voice from a Bluetooth speaker? Not so much. ClawTutor can now speak quiz reminders, screen time warnings, and more.

item.title: Teaching How They Learn — VARK Learning Types
item.description: Not every kid learns the same way. ClawTutor now detects each child's learning style and adapts its teaching approach — visual, auditory, read/write, or kinesthetic.

item.title: Meet Melody — Music Tutoring with a Real MIDI Keyboard
item.description: ClawTutor's third subject is live. A second Raspberry Pi reads a USB MIDI keyboard, sends notes via MQTT, and an AI tutor named Melody gives real-time feedback via WhatsApp.

item.title: Gaming Consoles Are Now Under Control
item.description: Nintendo Switch and PlayStation are now fully wired into ClawTutor. DNS blocking covers online play — and a smart plug closes the local-game loophole.

item.title: Why I Built ClawTutor — And Why I'm Open-Sourcing It
item.description: It started with a simple, daily argument. My kids wanted screen time; I wanted them to study first. After one too many negotiations I decided to outsource the whole thing to an AI tutor.
